mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously whenever the rate environment supports lower cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 2.33% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism activates only when unemployment crosses above the 4.0% threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.7% is below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand weakness rather than sector-specific shocks.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1446k units, RSAFS at 683.3B dollars
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of dual-decline instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism operates as a short-horizon tactical signal reflecting immediate earnings revision risk.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 104.5464, marginally above trigger
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO < 104.0 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration that precedes equity market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both indicators to move adversely, confirming broad labor market stress rather than compositional shifts.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in dual-stress instances are positive in > 55% of cases, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously in expansion regimes where growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 19699.465B (2017 dollars), asof t-5mo
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during positive GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.0% and acceleration in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both monetary and price pressures to align, confirming sustained tightening rather than temporary rate moves.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of dual-tightening instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1400k units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates as a leading indicator of broader demand contraction in late-cycle environments.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1400k units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1446k units, marginally above trigger
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 1400k are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
